turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Good morning, doctor. I’m here for my blood pressure follow-up. I’ve also been getting these headaches on and off for about two weeks.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Good morning. Let’s go through it carefully. Tell me about the headaches first. Where do you feel them, and what do they feel like?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Mostly across my forehead or at the back of my head. It’s a dull ache, not very strong, but it worries me.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: I understand. Are they sudden or severe, or more mild and gradual? Any blurred vision, vomiting, weakness, numbness, or trouble speaking?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, none of that. They come and go. Sometimes I notice them after a long day at work.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That’s helpful. Have you checked your blood pressure at home recently? And have you missed any doses of your amlodipine or hydrochlorothiazide?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I checked it twice at the pharmacy. It was around 145 over 92 one day and 150 over 94 the next. I’ve missed one tablet maybe last week, but otherwise I take them.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thank you. Any side effects from the tablets, like swelling of the ankles, dizziness, or needing to pass urine a lot?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No swelling. I do get up to urinate more because of the water pill, but nothing major. I feel fine otherwise.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Alright. Let me ask a few general questions. How is your sleep, stress, caffeine intake, and alcohol on weekends?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Sleep is not great when work is busy. I do drink two coffees a day, maybe three some days. On weekends I might have a few drinks socially.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: And with exercise and food, what does a normal week look like for you?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I sit most of the day at work. I try to walk three times a week, but not always. Food could be better, honestly. I do like salty snacks.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That’s honest, and useful. Any chest pain, shortness of breath, palpitations, or leg swelling?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, none of those. Just the headaches and the high readings.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Do you know your usual blood pressure when it is well controlled, and have you had any recent blood tests done for kidney function or electrolytes?

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Usually it is better than this, maybe around 130s over 80s. I had bloods last year, I think, but not recently.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Alright. Your readings are a bit above target, but not dangerously high today from what you’ve described. The headaches sound more like tension-type or stress-related headaches than an emergency cause. I’d like to check your blood pressure here, review your medication use, and arrange routine blood tests. We can also talk about tightening up salt, caffeine, alcohol, and exercise habits, and if the clinic reading is still high, we may need to adjust treatment. If the headache becomes severe, sudden, or comes with vision changes, weakness, confusion, chest pain, or shortness of breath, seek urgent care. Otherwise, let’s examine you now and make a follow-up plan.
